_about: Gold answer key for grading the assistant's ANSWERS. 'gold_facts' are verified against primary sources (see docs/sources.md) — they are the ground truth a human grades against. The harness eval_answers.py grades each live answer automatically: grounding (no fabricated/un-surfaced citations), must_cite / forbid_cite, citation recall vs expect_cite, required fact phrases, out_of_scope, and clarifying questions. Optional --judge runs an LLM faithfulness check (every claim supported by the cited rules).

cases.question: May I install a PV system, a 22 kW wallbox and a heat pump on my single-family home?

gold_facts.fact: Roof-parallel PV on a single-family home in Lower Saxony is verfahrensfrei (no building permit).
A wallbox above 11 kW (e.g. 22 kW) requires grid-operator approval; up to 11 kW only registration.
Wallbox and heat pump above 4.2 kW fall under §14a EnWG (controllable; reduced grid fee in return).
PV above 7 kWp requires an intelligent metering system; otherwise a 60% feed-in limit applies.
PV and storage must be registered in the Marktstammdatenregister within one month of commissioning.
Surplus feed-in tariff for ≤10 kWp (commissioning Feb–Jul 2026) is 7.78 ct/kWh.

cases.question: Which rules apply to my small 5 kWp PV system?

gold_facts.fact: 5 kWp is below the 7 kWp threshold → the smart-meter obligation must NOT be asserted as required here.
No wallbox is planned → wallbox approval rules must NOT appear.

cases.question: Ich möchte meine bestehende PV-Anlage erweitern — was muss ich beachten?

gold_facts.fact: For an existing-system expansion the assistant must first ask for the commissioning date (rules changed over time).

cases.question: What garden insurance do I need for a trampoline?

gold_facts.fact: Out of scope → the answer must recommend a specialist and cite NOTHING (no fabrication).
